chapter_title.ar: باب مَوْلِدِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ ( عليه السلام )
chapter_title.en: Chapter 116 – Nazool (Arrival) of Al-Husayn Bin Ali<sup>asws</sup>

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/367)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/367)
text: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ إِدْرِيسَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ إِدْرِيسَ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْأَوْدِيِّ قَالَ لَمَّا قُتِلَ الْحُسَيْنُ ( عليه السلام ) أَرَادَ الْقَوْمُ أَنْ يُوطِئُوهُ الْخَيْلَ فَقَالَتْ فِضَّةُ لِزَيْنَبَ يَا سَيِّدَتِي إِنَّ سَفِينَةَ كُسِرَ بِهِ فِي الْبَحْرِ فَخَرَجَ إِلَى جَزِيرَةٍ فَإِذَا هُوَ بِأَسَدٍ فَقَالَ يَا أَبَا الْحَارِثِ أَنَا مَوْلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فَهَمْهَمَ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ حَتَّى وَقَفَهُ عَلَى الطَّرِيقِ وَ الْأَسَدُ رَابِضٌ فِي نَاحِيَةٍ فَدَعِينِي أَمْضِي إِلَيْهِ وَ أُعْلِمُهُ مَا هُمْ صَانِعُونَ غَداً

قَالَ فَمَضَتْ إِلَيْهِ فَقَالَتْ يَا أَبَا الْحَارِثِ فَرَفَعَ رَأْسَهُ ثُمَّ قَالَتْ أَ تَدْرِي مَا يُرِيدُونَ أَنْ يَعْمَلُوا غَداً بِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يُرِيدُونَ أَنْ يُوطِئُوا الْخَيْلَ ظَهْرَهُ قَالَ فَمَشَى حَتَّى وَضَعَ يَدَيْهِ عَلَى جَسَدِ الْحُسَيْنِ ( عليه السلام ) فَأَقْبَلَتِ الْخَيْلُ فَلَمَّا نَظَرُوا إِلَيْهِ قَالَ لَهُمْ عُمَرُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ لَعَنَهُ اللَّهُ فِتْنَةٌ لَا تُثِيرُوهَا انْصَرِفُوا فَانْصَرَفُوا .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad said, ‘Abu Kureyb and Abu Saeed Al Ashajja narrated to me saying, ‘Abdullah Bin Idrees narrated to us, from his father Idrees Bin Abdullah Al Awdy who said,

‘When Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup> was killed, the people (army) wanted the cavalry horses to trample him<sup>asws</sup>. So Fizza<sup>as</sup> said to (Syeda) Zaynab<sup>as</sup>: ‘O my<sup>as</sup> chieftess!’ A ship broke in the sea, so it came out to an island, and there he was with a lion. So he said, ‘O Abu Haris! I am a slave of Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>!’ So it murmured to him until he paused upon the road, and the lion was crouching nearby. So leave me<sup>as</sup> to go to it and let it know what they are thinking of doing tomorrow’.

So he (the narrator) said, ‘So she<sup>as</sup> went to it and said: ‘O Abu Al-Haris!’ So it raised its head. Then she<sup>as</sup> said: ‘Do you know what they are intending of doing tomorrow with Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>? They are intending that the cavalry horses trample upon his<sup>asws</sup> back’. So it went until it placed its paws upon the body of Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup>. So the cavalry horses came over, but when they looked at it, Umar Bin Sa’d, may Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Curse him, said to them, ‘A strife! Do not raise it. Leave!’ So they left’.
en.sarwar: 6. Ahmad ibn Muhammad has narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Hassan from Muhammad ibn ‘Isa ibn ‘Ubayd from Ali ibn Asbat from Sayf ibn ‘Amira from Muhammad ibn humran who has said the following. “Abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) has said, ‘When all that happened to al-Husayn had happened, the angels wept and cried before Allah and said, “How such thing would happen to al-Husayn Your chosen one and the grand son of Your Prophet?” The Imam (a.s.) has said that Allah then showed to them the shadow of Al-Qa’im (the one who will rise with Divine Authority) and said, “Through him I will take My revenge.”
